ocr.text: In the analysis of birth data by region of mother's birth, the UK reports 1,375,306 males born and 1,304,933 females born,
yielding a sex ratio of 1.054 and an odds ratio of 1 with no confidence interval or p value available. In contrast, outside the UK,
there are 446,166 males and 425,539 females, resulting in a sex ratio of 1.048, an odds ratio of 0.995, and a 95% confidence
interval of 0.99 ŌĆō 1.00 with a p value of 0.035. When broken down by continent, Europe shows 140,445 males and 133,254
females, with a sex ratio of 1.054, odds ratio of 1.00, a confidence interval of 0.99 - 1.01, and a p value of 0.992. Africa has
99,771 males and 96,422 females, a sex ratio of 1.035, odds ratio of 0.98, confidence interval of 0.97 - 0.99, and a p value of
<0.001. The Americas and the Caribbean report 29,145 males and 27,435 females, achieving a sex ratio of 1.062, odds ratio of
1.01, a confidence interval of 0.99 - 1.02, with a p value of 0.350.
